controls.label: Does your business have a ML/TF Risk Assessment process?
controls.remediation: Your organisation does not have a ML/TF Risk Assessment process in place. You must establish a documented ML/TF Risk Assessment process to meet your AML/CTF obligations. Review the controls below and implement the required procedures before completing this assessment.

controls.label: Does your ML/TF Risk Assessment systematically consider all 7 prescribed risk factors when evaluating customer and service risk?
controls.detail-label: Describe how your risk assessment process incorporates each of the 7 prescribed risk factors
controls.remediation: Your ML/TF Risk Assessment does not incorporate all 7 prescribed risk factors required by the AML/CTF Rules. You must assess customer types (including beneficial owners and politically exposed persons), sources of funds and wealth, nature and purpose of business relationships, control structure of non-individual customers, types of designated services, delivery methods, and foreign jurisdictions, as mandated by rules 4.1.3(1) through 4.1.3(7).

checklist-items.label: Customer types (including beneficial owners and politically exposed persons)
Customers' sources of funds and wealth
Nature and purpose of business relationship with customers
Methods by which designated services are delivered
Foreign jurisdictions with which the entity deals

controls.label: Do you only apply beneficial owner and PEP requirements (rules 4.4.3(5), 4.4.5(5), Part 4.12, Part 4.13) to customers who became customers after 1 June 2014?
controls.remediation: Your ML/TF Risk Assessment applies beneficial owner and PEP identification requirements incorrectly. These requirements (rules 4.4.3(5), 4.4.5(5), and Parts 4.12 and 4.13) should only apply to customers who became customers after 1 June 2014, as specified in rule 4.1.6.

controls.label: When you suspect a customer is not who they claim to be, or have doubts about the adequacy of KYC information, do you take reasonable measures as soon as practicable to obtain and verify additional or updated KYC information?
controls.detail-label: Describe your process for obtaining and verifying additional KYC information when suspicions or doubts arise
controls.remediation: Your ML/TF Risk Assessment does not include adequate procedures for responding to suspicions or doubts about customer identity. When you suspect a customer is not who they claim to be, or have doubts about KYC information for the customer, beneficial owner, or agent, you must take reasonable measures as soon as practicable to obtain and verify additional or updated KYC information, as required by rules 6.1.2 and 6.1.3.

controls.label: When a suspicious matter reporting obligation arises for a pre-commencement customer, do you take appropriate action (carry out ACIP, collect additional KYC, or verify information) as soon as practicable?
controls.detail-label: Describe your process for handling pre-commencement customers when a suspicious matter reporting obligation arises
controls.remediation: Your ML/TF Risk Assessment does not adequately address pre-commencement customers when suspicious matter reporting obligations arise. You must carry out the applicable customer identification procedure (unless already done), collect additional KYC information, and verify that information from reliable and independent sources as soon as practicable, as required by rules 6.2.2 and 6.2.3.

controls.label: When a suspicious matter reporting obligation arises for a low-risk service customer, do you take appropriate action (carry out ACIP, collect additional KYC, or verify information) as soon as practicable?
controls.detail-label: Describe your process for handling low-risk service customers when a suspicious matter reporting obligation arises
controls.remediation: Your ML/TF Risk Assessment does not adequately address low-risk service customers when suspicious matter reporting obligations arise. You must carry out the applicable customer identification procedure (unless already done), collect additional KYC information, and verify that information from reliable and independent sources as soon as practicable, as required by rules 6.3.2 and 6.3.3.

groups.title: Process Confirmation
groups.description: Before answering the detailed compliance questions, confirm whether your organisation operates this business process.

groups.title: Prescribed Risk Factor Assessment
groups.description: The AML/CTF Rules require reporting entities to systematically consider 7 prescribed risk factors when assessing ML/TF risk. These factors form the foundation of your risk-based approach to customer due diligence.

groups.title: Transitional Requirements (Post-June 2014)
groups.description: Specific transitional provisions govern the application of beneficial owner and PEP requirements based on when customers commenced their relationship with your entity.

groups.title: Responding to Identity Suspicions or Doubts
groups.description: When you suspect a customer is not who they claim to be, or have doubts about the adequacy of previously obtained KYC information, you must take specific actions to resolve those concerns.

groups.title: Pre-Commencement Customers – Suspicious Matter Response
groups.description: Special provisions apply when a suspicious matter reporting obligation arises for a pre-commencement customer (one who became a customer before the AML/CTF Act commenced for your entity).

groups.title: Low-Risk Service Customers – Suspicious Matter Response
groups.description: When a suspicious matter reporting obligation arises for a low-risk service customer (one receiving a designated service with reduced identification requirements), you must take specific actions to verify their identity.
